data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at close
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.79% at 83,277.15, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index advanced 0.83% to 25,682.75, snapping a two-session losing streak, due to bottom fishing in heavyweight HDFC Bank and Reliance Industries as well as the broader market.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee dropped 0.02% versus the U.S. dollar to 90.6500, as usual dollar demand met offsetting inflows, keeping the currency largely rangebound at the start of the week.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 98.6950 rupees, with the yield up 1 bp at 6.6642%, following a surge in U.S. Treasuries and comfortable domestic liquidity, though lingering supply concerns contained the advance.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 1 basis point at 5.49%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) dropped 5 bps to 6.025%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 4.65% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 4.30%

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Weighs Renewed Talks With Paramount
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD, Financials) is reportedly considering reviving sale talks with Paramount Skydance after receiving an amended takeover proposal, according to Bloomberg.

Board members are evaluating whether Paramount's revised offer could provide a superior transaction compared with the company's existing agreement with Netflix (NFLX). No final decision has been made, and Warner Bros. Discovery remains bound by its current deal structure.

Earlier this month, Paramount Skydance added a "ticking fee" of up to $650 million every quarter starting after 2026 to its $108 billion offer. This is in case regulatory approvals slow down the closure of the deal. The change is meant to make up for any timing concerns that could come up during antitrust and other regulatory assessments.

The newfound interest might cause further competition between Paramount and Netflix, which could lead to a second round of bidding. Investors will be very interested in how the board handles the different bids, especially since this is a big issue and will likely be looked at seriously by regulators.

Any change in discussions might have a big impact on what people think the value of Warner Bros. Discovery shares will be.

data.title: Israel 10Y Bond Yield Hits 30-month Low
data.description:
Israel 10 Year Government Bond Yield decreased to 3.67%, the lowest since July 2023.

Over the past 4 weeks, Israel 10Y Bond Yield lost 2.30 basis points, and in the last 12 months, it decreased 53.30 basis points.

data.title: WPTG To Acquire Saltycustoms In Southeast Asia
data.description:
White Pearl Technology Group AB :

  • WPTG TO ACQUIRE SALTYCUSTOMS, WITH OPERATIONS IN MALAYSIA AND SINGAPORE

  • TOTAL PURCHASE CONSIDERATION USD 500,000

data.title: India’s trading crackdown has further to go
data.description:
By Shritama Bose

The regulator barred lenders from financing proprietary trades from outfits like Jane Street, triggering a 9% fall in broker shares. Coupled with higher taxes, it signals India is keen to rein in excessive risk-taking in the $1.4 trln options market. More action is likely.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Shritama Bose on LinkedIn and X.

CONTEXT NEWS

Shares of capital markets-linked companies listed in Mumbai fell up to 9% on February 16 after the Reserve Bank of India tightened financing rules for proprietary traders. Stock exchange operator BSE dropped 9%, while brokers such as Groww owner Billionbrains Garage Ventures, Motilal Oswal and Angel One lost between 1% and 7%.

The central bank on February 13 said all bank credit facilities to brokerages will need to be fully secured. It also barred banks from lending money to brokers to fund securities purchases for their own use, including for proprietary trading.

data.title: ASPO: EBITA up 25% to EUR 36.5M; transformation and higher 2026 EBITA expected
data.description:
EBITA grew 25% to EUR 36.5 million in 2025, driven by Telko and Leipurin, while ESL Shipping faced weak coaster markets. Transformation continues with Leipurin's sale and potential ESL demerger or sale, and guidance points to higher EBITA in 2026.

Based on

data.title: Swiss Growth Should Accelerate in First Quarter of 2026 — Market Talk
data.description:
1129 GMT - The Swiss economy should pick up in the first quarter of 2026, Pantheon Macroeconomics economist Ankita Amajuri says in a note. GDP expanded by 0.2% in the fourth quarter of last year, rebounding from a 0.5% fall in the third quarter. "Looking ahead, surveys suggest growth will pick up in the first quarter as Swiss firms now have greater clarity on the tariffs applied to their exports to the U.S.," Amajuri says, penciling in 0.5% expansion. About 40% of Swiss goods exports to the U.S. were subject to a 39% tariff for most of the third quarter. Growth in the fourth quarter reflected muted expansion in the services sector, while Swiss industrial output stagnated, as net trade was likely a drag on growth, she says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

1030 GMT - U.S. companies' supply of euro-denominated and sterling denominated corporate bonds increased to a new high through the first six weeks of 2026, CreditSights analysts say in a note. U.S. tech giant Alphabet issued 5.5 billion pounds in sterling bonds last week. The heavy bond issuance caused euro credit spreads and sterling credit spreads to widen last week, the analysts say. Euro investment-grade credit spreads widened 2 basis points to 75bps and sterling investment-grade credit spreads widened 3bps to 82bps, they say. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0948 GMT - The European Central Bank's plan to expand its euro liquidity facilities is unlikely to affect the dollar's global dominance, Commerzbank's Thu Lan Nguyen says in a note. The ECB announced Saturday it would extend euro repo lines to nearly all foreign countries in a bid to boost the single currency's international role. However, in times of stress in the global financial market, dollars are in demand rather than euros, Nguyen says. Most global trade is conducted in dollars, meaning companies need the currency to settle their liabilities, she says. That's why central banks hold most foreign exchange reserves in dollars to maintain their economies' foreign trade in a crisis. "The currency that dominates international payments also dominates everything else." (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0942 GMT - Emerging market stocks and bonds look attractive as investors seek diversification from U.S. tech stocks, Edmond de Rothschild Asset Management's Benjamin Melman says in a note. Concerns about potential disruption to software businesses from AI are causing investors to sell tech-sector stocks and bonds. "After lagging for several years, [emerging market] stocks stand to benefit as investors reposition their portfolios away from the United States," Melman says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0941 GMT - Sterling could fall as upcoming data might support the case for the Bank of England to cut interest rates again in March, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. Data on Tuesday should show a further cooling in the labor market and slower wage growth while data Wednesday are expected to show inflation eased in January, he says. Moreover, U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer remains vulnerable too, with betting markets assigning about a 70% chance he will step down by end of June. Sterling could "face depreciation episodes whenever Starmer's political position deteriorates," Pesole says. The euro falls 0.1% to 0.8687 pounds and ING expects it to reach 0.88 pounds in coming months. Versus the dollar, sterling trades flat at $1.3653. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0914 GMT - India's central bank is expected to stand pat until at least the end of 2027, according to Nomura research analysts. Stronger inflation under the new CPI series and a more positive growth outlook mean the Reserve Bank of India is unlikely to cut rates soon, they write in a note. The inflation trajectory is largely unchanged, and the new CPI series isn't expected to alter the RBI's view that underlying inflation remains benign and well-anchored to its medium-term target of 4%, the analysts add. Also, the trade deals with the U.S. and the EU, India's "expansionary" budget and the lagged effects of past rate cuts all set the stage for stronger GDP growth in FY 2027, they say. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0909 GMT - U.K. labor market, inflation and retail sales data due to be released this week could signal the likely date for the Bank of England's next interest-rate cut, Tickmill Group's Patrick Munnelly says in a note. Any further weakness in the labor market data and deceleration in inflation could reinforce market expectations of a rate cut in March, he says. Markets price in a 65% probability of a BOE rate cut in March, LSEG data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0902 GMT - Oil prices are broadly steady ahead of a second round of talks between the U.S. and Iran this week. Brent crude rises 0.1% to $67.60 a barrel, while WTI is flat at $62.30 a barrel after posting weekly losses last week. "Absent any Middle East supply disruption, the scope for a sustained move above $70 appears limited, given continued emphasis on ample supply and indications that some OPEC members see room to resume output increases in April," analysts at Saxo Bank say. Traders are also keeping a close eye on the U.S.-brokered talks between Russian and Ukrainian officials aimed at ending the four-year war. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0854 GMT - The European Central Bank's move to boost the euro's global role is positive for the single currency, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. The ECB on Saturday announced plans to extend its euro repo lines to nearly all foreign central banks, making it easier to secure funding in euros during periods of financial stress. ECB President Christine Lagarde said at the last meeting that euro globalization doesn't necessarily imply a stronger currency. However, in an environment where dollar confidence is eroding, euro globalization could boost the euro versus the dollar over the medium term, Pesole says. The exchange rate is "strictly tied to capital rotation from the U.S. to Europe." The euro trades flat at $1.1863. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0831 GMT - Yields on U.K. government bonds, or gilts, are steady in low volume trading as U.S., Chinese, and South Korean markets are closed for public holidays. Investors await key economic data, including U.K. inflation data and U.S. GDP data due to be released on Wednesday and Friday, respectively. Ten-year gilt yields are little changed at 4.404%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0828 GMT - Eurozone government bond yields edge lower in early trade, awaiting fresh drivers. U.S. markets are closed Monday, there is no eurozone government bond supply and the data calendar is thin. "Bunds may take a breather amid stabilising risk sentiment and the U.S. holiday, but the constructive duration backdrop is likely to extend ahead of the data reality check on Friday," Commerzbank's Rainer Guntermann says in a note. On Friday, flash estimate French, German and eurozone purchasing manager indices will be released for February. The 10-year German Bund yield edges down 0.7 basis points to 2.750%, while the 10-year French OAT yield falls 1.3 basis points to 3.331%, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0750 GMT - U.K. housebuilders are turning cautious as the sector shifts into a buyers' market, with homes for sale reaching an 11-year high in February, RBC Capital Markets analysts Anthony Codling and Oliver Dyson say in a note. According to Rightmove's latest report, asking prices remain virtually flat for the month and are unlikely to surprise housebuilders, the analysts say. Incentive levels remain high and sales rates have yet to bounce into the spring selling season, RBC says. On a weighted average year-on-year basis, regional house price trends in the month benefit Gleeson, Persimmon and Taylor Wimpey the most with Berkeley, Crest Nicholson and Vistry seen as benefiting the least, RBC says. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: WPTG to acquire Saltycustoms, with operations in Malaysia and Singapore
data.description:
Stockholm, Sweden, 2026-02-16 – White Pearl Technology Group AB (WPTG), a Swedish global digital transformation and IT services company, today announces that it has signed a Letter of Intent (LOI) to acquire Saltycustoms, a corporate merchandise, design, and digital fulfilment company headquartered in Southeast Asia, with operations in Malaysia and Singapore. The total purchase consideration for the proposed transaction is USD 500,000, of which USD 300,000 is to be paid in cash and USD 200,000 in WPTG shares, payable in three instalments, at closing, after 12 months and after 24 months provided that targets are met, and all subject to customary due diligence and final agreements.

The proposed acquisition represents a strategic step in strengthening WPTG’s Digital Transformation and Digital Commerce portfolio, while expanding its footprint in high-growth Southeast Asian markets.

Saltycustoms provides end-to-end corporate merchandise solutions, including design, production, e-store solutions, and global fulfilment. The company serves a broad base of multinational customers and has supported major global brands including Amazon Web Services and Citibank. The company positions itself as a full-service merchandise partner, offering design, manufacturing, and worldwide distribution services for clients ranging from startups to multinational corporations.

The transaction is expected to unlock value for WPTG through several strategic benefits:

  • Expansion of digital commerce capabilities. Saltycustoms’ e-store and fulfilment solutions complement WPTG’s existing digital transformation and customer experience offerings, enabling end-to-end digital merchandising and branded commerce platforms.
  • Access to global enterprise clients. Saltycustoms has delivered merchandise and fulfilment solutions to leading international customers, including Amazon Web Services and Citibank, creating cross-sell opportunities for WPTG’s broader IT services and digital transformation portfolio.
  • Southeast Asia growth platform. The acquisition strengthens WPTG’s presence in Malaysia and Singapore, two strategic markets in Southeast Asia, and provides a base for regional expansion.
  • High-margin, asset-light service model. Saltycustoms operates a design-, technology-, and fulfilment-led model with global logistics capabilities, supporting scalable and recurring revenue streams.

Marco Marangoni, CEO of White Pearl Technology Group, commented: “The proposed acquisition of Saltycustoms fits directly into our strategy of building a global digital transformation platform with strong vertical capabilities. Saltycustoms gives us a proven digital merchandise and fulfilment engine, access to global enterprise clients, and an immediate presence in Southeast Asia. We see significant cross-selling opportunities between their client base and WPTG’s broader digital, cloud, and IT services portfolio.”

For more information, please contact:

Peter Ejemyr, Vice President Investor Relations

White Pearl Technology Group

Email:ir@whitepearltech.com

Phone/WhatsApp: +46 733 611 000

The company's Certified Adviser is Amudova AB, email:info@amudova.se.

About White Pearl Technology Group:

White Pearl Technology Group AB (WPTG) is a Swedish global technology company specialising in digital transformation solutions. With a presence in over 20 countries and a team of 950 experts, WPTG helps organisations navigate the complexities of the digital age, offering services ranging from ICT and system integration to business software and digital innovation. The company is listed on Nasdaq First North (WPTGB) in Stockholm, Sweden and on OTCQX (WPTGF) in the U.S.

Attachments

WPTG to acquire Saltycustoms, with operations in Malaysia and Singapore

data.title: REG - Reabold Resources - West Newton Update
data.description:
RNS Number : 1867T Reabold Resources PLC 16 February 2026  

16 February 2026

Reabold Resources plc

("Reabold" or the "Company")

West Newton Update

Reabold Resources plc, the investing company focused on developing strategic gas projects for European energy security, confirms the following update on the West Newton gas field, located within PEDL183 (the "Licence"), onshore UK in East Yorkshire.

England's environmental regulator, the Environment Agency ("EA"), has issued Rathlin Energy (UK) Limited ("Rathlin"), operator of the Licence, the variation to the permit for the West Newton 'A' Well Site allowing for the recompletion works to be carried out at the West Newton A-2 ("WNA-2") well subject to certain pre-operational conditions. This is a key regulatory milestone on the path toward development and eventual production of the West Newton gas field.

The Company believes that the planned recompletion of the WNA-2 well is a low risk and low cost activity that will further derisk the project and provide important information in optimising future production wells.

The Company is confident that West Newton will prove to be an important strategic asset to the UK as the country looks to secure domestic energy supply and affordable energy.

Reabold holds a c.69.9% economic interest in West Newton and PEDL 183 via its c.79.8% shareholding in Rathlin, which, in turn, has a 66.67% interest in PEDL 183. In addition, Reabold has a 16.665% direct licence interest in PEDL 183.

Rathlin's full announcement can be viewed here: https://www.rathlin-energy.co.uk/latest-update/

Sachin Oza, Co-CEO of Reabold commented:

"We are delighted to announce this important regulatory milestone, which represents a major step forward in unlocking the full value of West Newton that we believe is a significant and strategic natural gas resource. The EA's approval underscores the diligent and careful work undertaken for the planned recompletion works. We look forward to providing further updates as the project progresses toward development and production, which would see it contribute meaningfully to the UK's domestic energy security."

Engage with the Reabold management team directly by asking questions, watching video summaries and seeing what other shareholders have to say. Navigate to our Interactive Investor hub here: https://reabold.com/s/2b4e66

For further information, please contact:

Investor questions on this announcement

We encourage all investors to share questions

on this announcement via our investor hub

https://reabold.com/link/eY2gYr

Reabold Resources Plc

Stephen Williams

Sachin Oza

Via our Investor Hub

https://reabold.com/ 

Cavendish - Nominated & Financial Adviser and Broker

Neil McDonald

Pearl Kellie

+44 (0) 20 7220 0500

Camarco

Billy Clegg

Rebecca Waterworth

Sam Morris

+44 (0) 20 3757 4980

Subscribe to our news alert service: https://reabold.com/auth/signup

Notes to Editors

Reabold Resources PLC is a UK-based upstream oil and gas investment company focused on generating returns through investment in low-risk energy projects with high potential upside. Investment activity is undertaken through strategic equity stakes in proven undeveloped gas discoveries with significant resources and near-term production potential, primarily across the UK and continental Europe. To support its investment strategy, Reabold balances proceeds from asset sales between shareholder returns and re-investment in new projects, with a focus on contributing to European energy security while achieving significant valuation uplift through clear monetisation pathways.
